profile.name: Vector Analysis Agent
profile.description: This agent performs vector-based spatial analysis and transformation using verified deterministic GeoPandas workflows for common operations and model-backed code workflows for more complex requests or failed shortcut validation. It supports operations such as clipping, joining, point-in-polygon counting, buffering, buffered point coverage calculations, intersecting, dissolving, area/length/centroid calculation, nearest-distance analysis, attribute filtering, geometry repair, format conversion, and aggregation. It can return GeoPackage or GeoJSON for spatial outputs and CSV for tabular outputs derived from the analysis.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Code-Driven Vector Spatial Analysis
skills.description: Uses deterministic GeoPandas/Pandas workflows for common joins, point-in-polygon counts, buffered point coverage percentages, buffers, clips, intersections, dissolves, geometry measurements, nearest-distance calculations, attribute filters, geometry repair, and format conversion when the operation can be confidently inferred and verified. For more complex requests, or when a deterministic artifact does not satisfy request-aware validation, it iteratively writes and executes Python code in a sandboxed environment to load, inspect, and analyze vector datasets using GeoPandas, Pandas, and Shapely.

constraints.spatial: Dependent on the coordinate reference systems (CRS) of the input datasets. The agent is instructed to project data to an appropriate CRS for distance calculations.
other: The autonomous reasoning and execution loop is capped at a maximum of 40 iterations.
Spatial results are saved as GeoPackage (.gpkg) by default unless GeoJSON is explicitly requested; tabular/attribute-only results are saved as CSV (.csv).
The geometry column must always be retained in spatial outputs.

Deterministic common-operation routing is attempted before model-backed code generation. A request-aware validation gate checks the deterministic artifact before delivery; if the artifact does not satisfy the request, the agent falls back to its model-backed workflow.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language geospatial task to the agent. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution.

input_datasets.description: Optional array of input datasets. Each item may be an HTTP/HTTPS URL, a server-accessible path, or a base64-encoded file object with filename, data, and optional mime_type.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response: 'URL' (default) or 'Encoded'.

primary_artifacts.description: Spatial vector result or tabular result produced by vector analysis.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model.

requested_skill.description: Advanced optional skill-selection hint. Most clients should omit this.

credentials.description: Provide one model execution credential in the request credentials object.

provenance.description: Includes llm_calls, tool_calls, token usage (input_tokens, output_tokens), number_of_artifacts, and script execution count in the response provenance section.

reproducibility.description: Returns the consolidated Python script used for execution in the script field. The script history is cleaned to remove redundant imports and file reads.

validation.description: Deterministic common-operation workflows validate requested output fields, join matches, point-in-polygon counts, non-empty spatial results, coverage fields, evidence of requested buffer/intersection/dissolve operations, geometry repair outcomes, and generated measurement/filter/nearest-distance fields before returning. More complex model-backed workflows still require downstream review for domain correctness.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile. No role-based access control is enforced.

operation_policy.description: Common vector operations use deterministic GeoPandas/Pandas workflows first, but deterministic outputs must pass request-aware validation before delivery. Complex or unverified workflows use Python code execution in a sandboxed environment with a maximum of 40 iterations.

review_policy.description: output_review_required is set to false. No human-in-the-loop validation is required.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the relevant research on geospatial AI agents.

extensions.description: This agent uses GeoPandas, Pandas, Shapely, and NumPy for vector spatial analysis. Deterministic fast paths support attribute joins, spatial joins, point-in-polygon feature counts, buffered point coverage percentages, buffering, clipping, intersections, dissolves, area/length/centroid fields, nearest-distance analysis, attribute filtering, geometry repair, and format conversion. The model-backed fallback maintains a persistent registry across code execution steps for intermediate variable storage and reuse.
